rag_rules: Preserve owner scope: interface-level contract content belongs on the component/type owner, while implementation-only content stays on the implementation owner.
Use the existing AADL model structure as the authority for owner, names, ports, subcomponents, connections, and data types during insertion.
generic_rules: Fuse annex content into the matching existing owner without changing non-annex architecture declarations.
Keep inserted text in a scope that can legally see every referenced symbol.
Preserve the surrounding model text and add only the minimal annex content needed.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE content is wrapped as an AADL annex block using `annex agree {** ... **};`.
anti_patterns: Do not place interface contracts in an implementation scope or implementation-only content in a type scope.
Do not move, rename, or create ports, subcomponents, connections, or properties as part of fusion.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
